assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Externally visible outputs remain defined and can change across conservative clocked observation windows.
The plan observes progress without assuming a fixed-cycle protocol or hidden internal state access.
clk_div exhibits a periodic toggling pattern whose period is an odd multiple of the input clock period (parameter NUM_DIV)
No X/Z values appear on clk_div during observation
cases.goal: Validate functional odd‑division behavior after a stable post‑reset initialization.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
Exact cycle count is not assumed due to unknown latency model.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Release reset if present and observe a small number of conservative clock edges for externally visible progress.
Release reset if present and observe a conservative number of clock edges for externally visible progress.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: clk_div toggles at a rate consistent with dividing clk by an odd number (parameter NUM_DIV) without assuming a fixed cycle count
No X or Z values appear on clk_div during steady‑state operation
cases.goal: Validate that the module produces a divided clock whose frequency corresponds to an odd divisor of the input clock.
notes: Exact latency is unknown; observation window is chosen conservatively large.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
preconditions: Reset has been released and the design is in a stable post‑reset state
stimulus_intent: Release reset and let the design run for many clock cycles while monitoring clk_div
timing_assumptions: Observe over a window of at least several multiples of the expected division period

expected_properties: clk_div remains stable (no toggling) while rst_n is asserted low
After deasserting rst_n, clk_div resumes toggling according to the odd division rule
cases.goal: Confirm correct handling of the active‑low reset and that reset polarity ambiguity does not cause unintended clk_div activity.
notes: Reset polarity for rst_n is known (active low); the other reset entry is ignored.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
stimulus_intent: Assert rst_n low for a few cycles, then release it while clocking clk, and observe clk_div behavior
timing_assumptions: Monitor clk_div for several cycles before, during, and after reset release

unresolved_items: Golden interface described clock 'clk_div', but the RTL port is not a scalar input-like signal.
